new_classes.definition_en: The geographic region of Scotland, United Kingdom, within whose territory Scotch whisky must be produced.

new_classes.label_en: Single malt Scotch whisky distillation process
new_classes.label_de: Herstellungsprozess für Single Malt Scotch Whisky
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process for producing single malt Scotch whisky: performed in pot stills, at a single distillery, from a fermented mash of only malted barley, occurring within Scotland.
A quality of a spirit production process indicating that distillation was carried out exclusively in traditional pot stills (Alembic/Pot Still), as required for single malt Scotch whisky.
A quality of a spirit indicating that it was produced entirely at one single distillery, as required for single malt Scotch whisky.

new_classes.label_de: Maische aus ausschließlich gemälzter Gerste Qualität
new_classes.definition_en: A quality of a spirit indicating that its fermented mash was made exclusively from malted barley, with no other cereal grains, as required for single malt Scotch whisky.

Single malt Scotch whisky: a spirit produced in pot stills at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash of only malted barley, with the production process occurring in Scotland. Classified under CN 2208 30 30.

coverage_explanation: All four key product-discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) geographic origin in Scotland (via ScotlandSite + occurs_in), (2) pot still distillation method (PotStillDistillationQuality on the process), (3) single distillery origin (SingleDistilleryOriginQuality on the process), and (4) fermented mash of only malted barley (MaltedBarleyOnlyMashQuality on the process). The producedBy restriction on the product class ties these together as sufficient conditions. A small coverage gap (0.05 deducted) exists because the legal text implicitly implies the product is a whisky (spirit category) which is captured by the parent class SpiritsLiqueursOtherSpiritousBeverages2208, but the sub-categorisation as 'whisky' specifically (as opposed to other spirits in 2208) is not further formalised with an explicit WhiskyQuality or grain-spirit quality. This is acceptable since the combination of malted-barley-only mash + pot still + single distillery + Scotland effectively defines single malt Scotch whisky uniquely within the CN.
